mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +0.5% in >60% of months where UNRATE remains above 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm a synchronized economic recovery, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals renewed manufacturing activity and inventory restocking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a win rate above 55% during early recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 500,000 units indicate severe credit market dysfunction and collapsing construction demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of months where HOUST remains below 500,000.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.5% while unemployment remains elevated, aggressive central bank liquidity provision offsets labor market weakness, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of triggered months or the interaction effect reverses sign during liquidity traps.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows or GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month declines in the consumer price index signal deflationary pressures that erode corporate profit margins and delay consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative CPI changes predict positive 5-day returns in >60% of observations during disinflationary periods.
